action.reasoning: Observation: The training script is still failing with a segmentation fault (exit code 139) after multiple attempts to fix the model implementation. The issue persists even after correcting the SchNet forward call to not pass the batch parameter. Analysis: The segmentation fault could be due to several factors: 1) Memory issues with the SchNet model when processing the data, 2) Issues with the input data format, 3) Potential bugs in the PyTorch Geometric library when using SchNet, 4) Problems with CUDA/GPU memory management. Since the SchNet model is complex and may have compatibility issues, I'll switch to a simpler but effective alternative: using a basic GNN with 3D coordinates. Decision: I'll implement a simpler model using basic PyTorch Geometric layers that can handle 3D coordinates effectively without the complexity of SchNet.
